home.title: PromptCost: how much does 1 AI prompt really cost?
home.description: See the real cost of an AI prompt (electricity, water, CO₂) by comparing it to everyday actions. Sourced numbers, no agenda.

intro.sub: One shower is worth 167,000 of them. A single AI prompt is tiny: add everyday actions and see how few it takes to burn through a whole year.

facts.f1: A text prompt ≈ 0.3 Wh: about an LED bulb on for ~2 minutes.
facts.f2: The cooling water for one prompt ≈ 0.3 mL: 5 drops.
facts.f3: A prompt's CO₂ ≈ 0.2 g: an email with an attachment weighs ~250× more.
facts.f4: A 5-minute shower is « worth » about 250,000 prompts in water.
facts.f5: A Paris → New York flight is « worth » millions of prompts in CO₂.

comparatif.title: Comparison
comparatif.subtitle: The « classic » way versus AI, side by side. Static, sourced data.

case1.title: Google search vs AI text query
case1.verdict: Comparable orders of magnitude: depending on the source, the gap ranges from « nearly identical » to « a few times more ». The range matters more than the verdict.

case2.title: Downloaded image vs AI-generated image
case2.verdict: Generating an image costs ~20 to 40× a text query. Downloading an existing image is near zero.

case3.title: 1 min of streaming video vs 1 text prompt
case3.verdict: One minute of HD video is worth several text prompts. A single AI text prompt is tiny, and volume is what builds the total.

sources.title: Sources & data
sources.intro: Every number on this site comes from one of these sources. Cross-referenced 2025-2026.

methodologie.title: Methodology
methodologie.intro: How we turn an everyday action into a « number of prompts ». Honest and bounded by ranges.
methodologie.q1: How much does an AI prompt consume?
methodologie.a1: For an average text prompt (GPT-4o class): ~0.3 Wh of electricity, ~0.3 mL of direct cooling water, ~0.2 g of CO₂. « Reasoning » queries or image generation cost far more.
methodologie.q2: How do I compute the « cost in prompts » of an action?
methodologie.a2: We divide the action's consumption by a prompt's: prompts = action value ÷ prompt cost. A 50 L shower ≈ 50,000 mL ÷ 0.3 mL ≈ 167,000 prompts.
methodologie.q3: Why 12,000 prompts per person per year?
methodologie.a3: It's a heavy-use assumption (≈ 33 prompts/day). A scale's budget = 12,000 × number of people. Your actions, though, stay counted for a single person: only the goal grows with the scale. That's how one person can be « worth » a whole group's yearly AI.
methodologie.q4: What are « virtual » water and CO₂?
methodologie.a4: For food and goods we count the production footprint (« virtual » water and CO₂), not just tap water. A burger « costs » mostly through farming, not the glass of water at the meal.
methodologie.q5: Why ranges instead of one exact figure?
methodologie.a5: Sources vary (power mix, measurement method, scope). We keep a credible median and show the range. Goal: honest orders of magnitude, not false precision. For instance, a flight may or may not include radiative forcing (×~1.9 per DEFRA/ADEME, CO₂ only for ICAO, plus one-way vs round-trip); beef ranges from ~60 to ~99 kg CO₂e/kg depending on farming.

apropos.title: About
apropos.p1: PromptCost puts the cost of an AI prompt in perspective. Neither doom nor denial: just sourced orders of magnitude you handle yourself.
apropos.p2: A prompt is tiny on its own. At the scale of the planet, the totals become dizzying. Both are true, scale is what counts.

legal.title: Legal notice
legal.p1: Editorial site for educational purposes. Numbers are indicative: sourced orders of magnitude, not certified measurements.
legal.p2: No personal data collected, no tracking cookies.

footprint.label: This site's footprint
footprint.method: This site used AI for in-depth research and a bit of development. All in, building it adds up to about 250 Wh, 0.25 L of water and 170 g of CO₂.
footprint.perVisit: And each visit? A few tenths of a Wh on lightweight static hosting: negligible, even with lots of traffic.

water.1: ≈ far less than a toilet flush

footer.disclaimer: Indicative data: sourced orders of magnitude.

glossary.metaTitle: Glossary: Wh, virtual water, radiative forcing… | PromptCost
glossary.metaDescription: The key terms to understand the cost of an AI prompt and everyday actions.
glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.intro: The key terms to understand the numbers on this site.

prompt.def: A request sent to an AI model. Site reference: an average text prompt ≈ 0.3 Wh, 0.3 mL of water and 0.2 g of CO₂.

wh.def: A unit of energy: running a 1 W device for 1 h. An LED bulb uses ~10 Wh per hour.

virtualWater.def: The water used to produce a good (farming, livestock, manufacturing), not just tap water.

co2e.def: CO₂ equivalent: all greenhouse gases expressed as their CO₂ impact.

radiative.def: Extra warming from flights at altitude. DEFRA/ADEME include it (×~1.9), ICAO counts CO₂ only.

reasoning.def: A model that thinks before answering. Far costlier than a text prompt: ~15 to 40 Wh.
